findings.finding: The readiness transition is delegated to a dedicated bos-mcp actor; the coordinator adds no GitHub REST/GraphQL dependency or credentials and leaves merge policy enforcement in the existing deployment-verifier role.

The deterministic operationId binds runId and exact headCommit, the pending handoff survives response loss, and retry recovery reuses the same identity while the durable bos-mcp receipt confirms the operation.

The coordinator requires an open, non-draft pull request at the expected HEAD before starting the only merge-capable role; stale HEAD, closed PR, and provider failure remain distinct errors that stop downstream merge and deployment.

Exact-commit evidence reports 277 tests passed with 2 skipped and make all passing formatting, specs, Credo, 100% coverage, and Dialyzer; targeted tests cover normal ordering, response-loss recovery without duplicate provider mutation, stale HEAD blocking, and distinct closed/provider rejection.

summary: Passed: the exact-HEAD draft-readiness step is correctly inserted as an idempotent bos-mcp boundary before policy merge, with durable recovery semantics, bounded failure states, and exact-commit validation.
